entities.name: KABULI CHICKPEA
entities.description: Kabuli chickpea (Cicer arietinum L.) with large seeds

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: Important trait in kabuli chickpea, specifically 100-seed weight

entities.name: 100-SEED WEIGHT
entities.description: Weight of 100 seeds, used as a measure of seed size; >40g and >50g are significant thresholds

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of product obtained from a crop

entities.name: MARKET PRICE
entities.description: The price at which kabuli chickpeas are sold

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Locally adapted, traditional varieties of chickpea

entities.name: BREEDING LINES
entities.description: Chickpea lines developed through breeding programs

entities.name: MORPHOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Observable characteristics of the chickpea plants

entities.name: ICRISAT GERMPLASM COLLECTION
entities.description: A collection of chickpea genetic resources maintained by ICRISAT

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: The different growing conditions in which the chickpeas were evaluated

entities.name: ICC 17109 (BLANCO SINALOA 92)
entities.description: A specific breeding line of kabuli chickpea originating from Mexico

entities.name: ICC 17452 (CUGA 288)
entities.description: A specific landrace of kabuli chickpea originating from Mexico

entities.name: MEXICO
entities.description: Country of origin for ICC 17109 and ICC 17452

entities.name: VEGETATIVE CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: Characteristics of the plant excluding reproductive parts

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: Yield based on the grain produced

relationships.weight: Consumers prefer kabuli chickpeas with larger seed size
Larger seed size leads to higher market price
ICRISAT germplasm collection contains breeding lines
Vegetative characteristics are more sensitive to stress than yield
Selection in unfavorable environments should be based on grain yield
Grain yield is composed of yield components
